name: aws-cost-optimizer
description: Comprehensive AWS cost analysis and optimization recommendations using AWS CLI and Cost Explorer
capabilities: Parse AWS Cost Explorer data for spending trends and anomalies
Identify idle EC2 instances with low CPU utilization
Find unattached EBS volumes, old snapshots, and unused Elastic IPs
Generate rightsizing recommendations based on CloudWatch metrics
Calculate potential savings from Reserved Instances and Savings Plans
Create cost breakdown reports by service, region, and resource tags
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 40 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is documentation containing AWS CLI command examples. Shell command substitutions are bash date formatting in CLI examples, not executable code. URLs are documentation references. Keyword matches for 'C2' and 'weak crypto' are false positives from 'CPU utilization' terminology. No actual code execution or network calls present.
valueStatement: Cloud costs often spiral out of control without proper monitoring. This skill identifies waste, analyzes spending patterns, and provides actionable cost reduction strategies using AWS native tools.
userTitle: Analyze and Optimize AWS Cloud Costs
limitations: Requires AWS CLI configured with appropriate IAM permissions
Analysis is read-only; deletion commands must be executed separately by the user
Does not automatically implement optimizations or make infrastructure changes
Cost data availability depends on AWS Cost Explorer being enabled in the account

useCases.title: Monthly Cost Review
useCases.description: Finance teams analyze monthly AWS spending, identify top cost drivers, and prepare reports for leadership on cloud expenditure trends.

useCases.title: Pre-Migration Assessment
useCases.description: DevOps engineers evaluate current AWS resource utilization before migration projects to identify and eliminate waste before moving workloads.

useCases.title: Cost Optimization Audit
useCases.description: Solutions architects conduct comprehensive audits to find optimization opportunities like unused resources, rightsizing candidates, and Reserved Instance gaps.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Cost Inquiry
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me AWS costs for the last 30 days broken down by service, and highlight any services with spending increases over 20% compared to the previous period.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick spending overview for routine monitoring

promptTemplates.title: Unused Resource Detection
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all unattached EBS volumes, unused Elastic IPs, and EC2 instances with average CPU utilization below 5% over the past 7 days. Calculate the monthly cost savings if these resources are removed or stopped.
promptTemplates.scenario: Identify quick wins for immediate cost reduction

promptTemplates.title: Rightsizing Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze my EC2 and RDS instances using CloudWatch metrics from the past 30 days. Recommend specific instance type changes for any resources consistently using less than 40% of their capacity, and estimate the cost difference.
promptTemplates.scenario: Deep optimization analysis for long-term savings

promptTemplates.title: Executive Cost Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a cost optimization report suitable for leadership that includes: total monthly spend, top 5 cost categories, identified waste with dollar amounts, recommended actions with estimated savings, and a 3-month cost trend visualization.

bestPractices: Always verify resources are truly unused before deletion by checking tags and consulting team members
Enable AWS Cost Anomaly Detection and set up budget alerts for proactive monitoring
Test rightsizing recommendations in non-production environments before applying to production workloads
antiPatterns: Deleting resources without verifying they are not used by disaster recovery or backup processes
Focusing only on cost reduction without considering performance and reliability impacts
Making optimization changes during business hours without a rollback plan

faq.question: What AWS permissions are required to use this skill?
faq.answer: Read-only permissions are needed: ce:GetCostAndUsage for Cost Explorer, ec2:Describe* for resource discovery, and cloudwatch:GetMetricStatistics for utilization data. No write permissions are required for analysis.

faq.question: Can this skill automatically delete unused resources?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides analysis and recommendations only. All deletion or modification commands must be reviewed and executed manually by the user to prevent accidental data loss.

faq.question: How far back can AWS Cost Explorer provide data?
faq.answer: AWS Cost Explorer retains up to 12 months of historical cost data. For older data, you would need to export reports to S3 and analyze them separately.

faq.question: Does this skill work with AWS Organizations?
faq.answer: Yes, when run from the management account with appropriate permissions, it can analyze consolidated billing data across all member accounts in the organization.

faq.question: What is the difference between BlendedCost and UnblendedCost metrics?
faq.answer: BlendedCost uses blended rates across accounts in an organization. UnblendedCost reflects the actual rates each account pays. Use UnblendedCost for accurate per-account cost analysis.

faq.question: How often should I run cost optimization analysis?
faq.answer: Run quick checks for unused resources weekly. Perform comprehensive analysis monthly for budget reviews. Conduct deep optimization audits quarterly or before major infrastructure changes.
